name: Tangy Salmon Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the can of salmon.
2. Heat the grapefruit juice to almost boiling, then sprinkle over the gelatine and stir gently until completely dissolved.
3. Mash the two types of smoked salmon into a bowl and beat in the yogurt, curd cheese or Quark, and 1½ teaspoons of ginger. Stir in the dissolved gelatine and allow the mixture to cool until it begins to set slightly.
4. Whisk the egg whites until they form stiff peaks. Fold these into the salmon mixture, then pour into a single 900ml (1½ pint) fish mold or two 450ml (¾ pint) molds. Place in the refrigerator and leave until completely set.
5. Peel the grapefruits and remove the pith from each segment. Combine the segments with the remaining ginger, walnut oil, and grapefruit juice in a bowl. Mix well and allow to stand for about 1 hour to marinade.
6. Arrange the salad leaves and avocado on serving plates. Carefully turn the mousse onto the plates using a gentle tilting motion.
7. Just before serving, arrange the marinated grapefruit segments and sprinkle with juices.
Cook's Note: To turn out the mousses more easily, first dip the molds in hot water to loosen them, then invert them onto the serving plates. Gently shake the plates from side to side; the mousses should drop out.
Serves 8-10. Approx. 130 kcals per serving
